stem: The function f is defined by f(x) = (−5)(3)x + 17. What is the y-intercept of the graph of y = f(x) in the xy-plane?
rationale:
Choice D is correct. The y-intercept of the graph occurs at x = 0. Substituting yields f(0) = (−5)(3)0 + 17 = (−5)(1) + 17 = 12. So the y-intercept is (0, 12).

Choices A and B isolate one piece of the expression. Choice C misuses the base 3.
